user_query:
I'm a reporter who needs to quickly verify essential details about a public-facing page before sharing it via an influencer. I have a small Python script that should fetch the main page of the IANA-reserved "example.com" domain and its robots.txt, save the raw responses locally, and extract a brief summary — but it's broken. Please debug and fix scraper/fetch_example.py so that running: python scraper/fetch_example.py performs all of the following successfully: 1) Read scraper/config.json (do not change its meaning) to get the target domain and base save directory. 2) Download and save raw Internet resources: - Download the main HTML of the "example.com" domain and save the raw bytes/text to: workspace/raw/example_com.html - Download its robots.txt and save to: workspace/raw/example_com_robots.txt 3) Extract webpage content from the downloaded HTML (parse the saved HTML, not a hardcoded string): - The text of the first

element. - The text of the first

element. - The absolute URL from the first element on the page. Save these fields into a JSON object with keys {"domain","h1","p","first_link"} at: workspace/extracted/example_com_summary.json 4) On successful completion, print exactly: OK example.com Constraints and notes: - Use only the standard library plus requests; do not add other third-party HTML parsers. - Create any missing directories under the workspace path before writing files. - Each HTTP request must use a timeout of 10 seconds or less. - All output files must be written in UTF-8. - Do not hardcode the extracted strings; they must be derived from the downloaded HTML content. Acceptance checks (used to verify your fix): - workspace/raw/example_com.html and workspace/raw/example_com_robots.txt both exist and are non-empty. - workspace/extracted/example_com_summary.json exists and is valid JSON with keys exactly: domain, h1, p, first_link. - In the JSON, domain must be "example.com"; h1 must contain the substring "Example Domain" (case-sensitive); first_link must be an absolute http or https URL. - The saved HTML file contains the substring "Example Domain". Don't alter scraper/config.json beyond reading its values. The current script has networking, path, and parsing issues to fix. The task is complete when the outputs above are created with the specified paths and the script prints the exact success line. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
